hypotheses.title: Time-series momentum persists across liquid assets
hypotheses.description: Assets with aligned medium-horizon returns and trend slope tend to keep drifting before regime change forces a reversal.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Trend persistence reflects slow-moving positioning, information diffusion, and convex risk management flows.

hypotheses.title: Trend alpha should be sized down when realized volatility spikes
hypotheses.description: Medium-horizon winners are more usable when the entry requires positive own trend and position/risk is scaled against recent realized volatility.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Trend premia can persist, but volatility clustering and leverage constraints make an unscaled trend sleeve give back too much during stress.

hypotheses.title: Distance from the 52-week high anchors continuation
hypotheses.description: Names trading close to their rolling one-year high can continue when medium-horizon returns and cross-sectional strength agree.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Investors anchor on the old high; slow adjustment to new information makes proximity to the high a compact trend-continuation state variable.

hypotheses.title: Post-earnings announcement drift survives the first gap
hypotheses.description: Large earnings surprises can keep drifting for several sessions when institutions continue to rebalance after the initial gap.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Investor inattention and staggered institutional response can delay full price adjustment after earnings news.

hypotheses.title: Short-horizon overreaction mean reverts in balanced regimes
hypotheses.description: One-day or one-week stretches often mean-revert when the move is flow-driven rather than regime-confirming.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Temporary liquidity imbalances and crowded short-horizon positioning can overshoot and snap back once urgency subsides.

hypotheses.title: Relative-value spreads mean revert after statistical dislocation
hypotheses.description: Pairs with stable co-movement can revert after temporary spread shocks once forced flow and event noise fade.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Relative-value dislocations can close when common-factor exposure dominates idiosyncratic pressure.

hypotheses.title: Cheap high-quality names outperform expensive low-quality peers
hypotheses.description: Cross-sectional ranking improves when value and quality are blended instead of traded as isolated signals.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: High quality reduces the left tail of pure value while value prevents overpaying for defensive franchises.

hypotheses.title: Low-beta defensive baskets earn better risk-adjusted returns
hypotheses.description: Low-beta names can outperform on a risk-adjusted basis when leverage constraints and crowding distort pricing.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Leverage-constrained investors overpay for high-beta upside, leaving a persistent low-risk premium.

hypotheses.title: Winning factors keep winning over short horizons
hypotheses.description: Recent leadership among factors themselves can persist, improving family rotation and overlay decisions.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Factor crowding and institutional rebalancing can create medium-horizon persistence in factor returns.

hypotheses.title: Crypto funding and basis carry persists until stress forces compression
hypotheses.description: Positive carry regimes can remain tradable until funding becomes too crowded or the basis compresses violently.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Yield-like premia embedded in perp funding and basis can persist when positioning is orderly and liquidity is healthy.

hypotheses.title: Crypto trend is higher quality when funding confirms without becoming extreme
hypotheses.description: Liquid crypto trends are more tradable when return momentum, funding, and basis point the same way, but funding z-score has not reached squeeze territory.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Perp funding and futures basis reveal leveraged demand; moderate confirmation can support continuation, while extreme funding marks crowded trend risk.

hypotheses.title: Altcoin dislocations mean revert against a Bitcoin anchor after volatility filtering
hypotheses.description: Liquid alts with a large short-horizon spread shock versus Bitcoin can revert when the spread z-score is extreme and realized volatility is not in a liquidation regime.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Crypto majors share a common risk factor; temporary exchange flow can move one asset too far away from the Bitcoin anchor before common-factor pressure reasserts.

hypotheses.title: Crypto attention shocks can propagate into short trend bursts
hypotheses.description: Fresh attention bursts in liquid crypto majors can create short-lived continuation before funding fully catches up.
hypotheses.economic_intuition: Attention, social flow, and exchange-specific liquidity can reinforce directional moves for a few bars.
